label: Bloodrift
description: A vertical fortress-city carved into the cliffs of a massive canyon on Arrur, central to war and trade.
Population: ~30,000 hardened souls. Mostly orcs, a scattering of halflings, dwarves, and a few desperate humans.
Geography: Built into cliff walls and along crumbling ledges descending into the Bloodrift canyon — vertical living is a necessity.
Economy: Imports high-end weaponry, magic artifacts, and atmospheric processors. Exports war material, mercenary contracts, and scavenged ancient tech.
Culture: Aggressive pragmatism. Honor is measured in how much you can haul up from the Rift and how many times you climb back down again.

containsPlace.label: The Mawport
containsPlace.description: The spaceport plateau — fortified, heavily militarized, ringed with anti-orbital guns.

containsPlace.label: The Chainmarkets
containsPlace.description: Suspended markets built into iron scaffolding along the cliffs. You buy and sell midair.

containsPlace.label: The Clutch
containsPlace.description: The dense, tangled lower city where the poorest dwell in nets, cages, and shanties.

containsPlace.label: The Redsteel Hall
containsPlace.description: The main gathering hall for mercenary companies — contracts are fought over with ritualized knife duels.

containsPlace.label: The Blackcradle
containsPlace.description: Deepest known cavern accessible from Bloodrift, rumored to house an entire forgotten race — or worse.

attitude: Bloodrift trades blood for steel and laughter for guns.
To survive here, you climb higher than your enemies and cut the ropes behind you.
